reviews.text:
If it were not for Patrick’s emotional support and legal skill I would not have made it through what has been the darkest time in my life. I was accused of a serious crime I did not commit and it threw my personal and professional life into complete disarray. The police and the prosecution would not listen to me at all, but Patrick did. He knew there was no way I could have done what I was accused of and he vowed to fight for me until the end to get these charges completely dismissed. He fought the prosecution tenaciously, showing them over time why their case was weak, ultimately getting the case completely dismissed before trial.

Patrick is incredibly well respected in the community and knowledgeable on both sides of the law. He used to work in the prosecutor’s office for many years and knows the people in the office and their procedures. This, I think, really helps him “think like a prosecutor” and helps him get ahead of them. While my case did not end up at trial, I was comfortable going to trial with him because he has so much trial experience. Over 300 cases! I always felt like he would fight for me until the end. Finally, everyone seems to know him in the law community. I had to consult with other lawyers for help on the professional side and everyone had only glowing things to say about Patrick. They were even more confident than me that my case would get resolved favorably since they knew Patrick’s reputation so well! I think this strong reputation is invaluable and part of the reason we were able to resolve my case without going to trial.

Patrick is an independent lawyer, but he has a very professionally run law office. For a one-man operation, everything ran incredibly smoothly; no appointments got mixed up, we were able to get in touch with him easily, and everything flowed very well. I think this is due to his assistants, Cody and Emily. They were amazing. They were always on top of everything, whether that was getting things scheduled, organizing paperwork, or preparing exhibits for trial. They seem very committed to Patrick and constantly talked about what a great person and lawyer Patrick is. Having people like this on his team made me feel more at ease.

While he is a top-notch lawyer, I think his best quality is that he really cared about me and securing a good outcome for my case. Even when I wanted to give up, at my lowest point, he kept encouraging me. He gave me the strength to fight. He had faith in me even when I didn’t have it in myself. This was truly the only thing that got me through. I really feel this is a key difference in other lawyers that only care about money. Some would have just accepted a plea, but he knew that we could beat this, he convinced me that we could, and he was right! I am so thankful to have had him represent me.

Patrick always made me feel comfortable and informed in any situation. Patrick is down to Earth; he is a husband and a father, which made him relatable and more than just a lawyer. He has a clear way of explaining things. I am not originally from this country, and I have never been involved in any legal issue before. He broke everything down simply, step by step, and was always very patient with me whenever I asked questions that were probably pretty stupid. He was also a man of his word. Anytime he said he was going to do something, whether file a motion, talk to the prosecution, or track down some lead, he did it and did it as quickly as possible. He was also clear and would not sugar coat anything. He told me plainly what was going to happen, what was possible and what wasn’t even if it wasn’t what I wanted to hear.

If I had to hire a lawyer in the future, I would hire him again in a heartbeat. That being said, I hope I never see him again in a court of law!

Review of Speaker Law Firm – Mishandling of Appeal in Conservatorship of BJH (COA 369128)

If you are considering retaining Speaker Law Firm or Liisa Speaker, I urge you to read this carefully. What I experienced was not just legal negligence — it was a betrayal of trust, professional abandonment, and outright misrepresentation.

After paying over $20,000 to Speaker Law Firm to handle my mother’s probate appeal, I was shocked to learn — the night before oral argument — that Liisa Speaker, the attorney listed on my appellate filings, would not be arguing my case. Instead, a newly licensed attorney, Krysten Hergert, who had passed the bar less than one month before the Application for Leave was filed, was substituted in without my knowledge or consent.

When I objected, Speaker’s response on the morning of the hearing was chilling:

“We are not rescheduling argument. Krysten has always been the main attorney assigned to your case.”
— Liisa Speaker, Jan. 7, 2025, 7:51 AM

This was a lie. The brief was signed by Liisa Speaker — not Hergert — and Krysten was not even licensed when the appeal began.

Throughout the case, I don’t recall ever speaking to any attorney. All communication was filtered through their office manager, Mark Forsythe. Liisa Speaker later admitted this was their policy:

“My intention in limiting attorney-client communication is to save clients time and costs.”
— Liisa Speaker, Jan. 9, 2025

This is unacceptable. For $20,000+, I was entitled to speak with a licensed attorney. Instead, I was assigned someone unqualified and kept completely in the dark.

I had provided them clear, documented evidence that:
•   The court’s judgment was based on a an outdated Guardian ad Litem report,
•   The transcript they filed misstated key facts (e.g., falsely stating I paid $40/hour to a caregiver instead of $20), The transcript they filed was not filed in the lower court
•   The accounting relied upon had already been denied by the probate court, and
•   My own attorney, Michael Caine, had submitted corrected accountings with full documentation.

Despite this, Speaker Law refused to supplement the record or correct the fraud. Their justification?

“We are not at all persuaded that the things you are jumping up and down about will make any kind of difference.”
— Liisa Speaker, Jan. 16, 2025

They even threatened me:

“If we withdraw and you file documents and lose the appeal, that will be 100% on you.”
— Liisa Speaker, Jan. 16, 2025

After the appeal was lost, a completely new attorney, Elizabeth Parker, emailed me for the first time — without ever having participated in my case — to say they were closing my file:

“We are closing our file and will be taking no further action.”
— Elizabeth Parker, April 14, 2025

I had previously warned Speaker Law that they were facilitating a fraud on the court and cited MCR 2.612(C)(1)(c)-(d) and MRPC 3.3(a)(3). They ignored it.

Their final response?

“We believe we have complied with all ethical and legal obligations.”
— Elizabeth Parker

No, they did not. They lied, abandoned their duties, and allowed a fraudulent record to destroy my appeal.

This wasn’t a mistake — it was malpractice.

⸻

Avoid Speaker Law Firm. They will smile, take your money, and leave you alone in the courtroom. If you’re facing serious legal stakes — especially involving your family or estate — this firm may cost you everything.

Cynthia Mifsud

reviews.text:
DO NOT
hire this man. Everything started out fine with him, but since the beginning of June when I left him some work to perform for me and started calling him in July and August to see if it was done, he would not return my calls, and he would not return any emails to me.
I have called him multiple times, with no return phone calls, and sent at least four to six emails to him with no response, plus I gave him a deposit to do this work.
If you don't want calls on Sunday...dont answer your phone. Maybe a good lawyer...terrible personal skills. Arrogant without listening.
reviews.ownerResponse:
I’m sorry you weren’t happy. My hours are listed on my website and google maps but I am available outside those hours for emergencies. I consider that a service many firms don’t provide not a ‘negative’.

You called on Sunday PM with a non-urgent matter concerning property in an area of the state I don’t practice in.
I spent 5 minutes listening but when I provided advice that contradicted what you read ‘on the internet’ and which you didn’t want to hear — and suggested you needed to contact an attorney in the area of the property somehow that was ‘terrible people skills’?

You do need to find a local attorney. That isn’t arrogance. It is nearly 40 years of experience.

August 14, 2023

To Whom It May Concern:

It has been almost two years since I first reached out to Atty Doug Chalgian for advice about guardianship/conservatorship for my fiancé who was hospitalized with Covid pneumonia. Sadly, during that time,  both my mom and fiancé transitioned to heaven within two months of each other. It was a very emotionally challenging time that also included a contested will, probate, and some ill-willed relatives.

Doug was very confident and supportive and later introduced me to Attorney Dan Hilker who quite honestly was a God send.  From the moment we began to work toward resolution, Dan dispelled many of the negative pre-conceived complaints about attorneys: neglectful, lack of communication, dishonesty, excessive fees…

Dan instead proved to be the complete polar opposite. He exhibited the utmost in integrity, professionalism, sincerity, communication and compassion. As friends and family journeyed with me through some very turbulent times, they also witnessed how helpful and honest Dan was in every way. So much so, that they have commented that when in need, Dan will be their attorney of choice.

As a mental health therapist by profession, I am fully aware of the power of effective listening.  Dan’s patience and willingness to really listen and know his client is remarkable. In addition, I know this is a bonus, but Dan has always tried to respond in a timely manner. This is very much appreciated due to my limited availability in between clients. What could be a stressful game of telephone tag, seems to always flow smoothly as a result of his attentiveness and organizational skills.

I cannot say enough about the wonderful staff at Chalgian and Tripp Law Offices ( Brandy Lowe and Nathanael Meredith) to mention a few. I am so grateful for their efficiency in communication and legal services. As we begin to wind down from quite a rollercoaster-like series of events, I am ever so grateful for having worked with such a caring firm and especially, such a phenomenal human being as Atty Dan Hilker.

Respectfully,
Teresa Traverso

reviews.text:
I’m fully aware that "Chuck" will likely respond defensively to this review, as accountability for himself and his firm is not his strong suit. That said, I will be echoing what many other reviewers have already said.

The attorney I was assigned repeatedly told me things like “we’ll file your motion tomorrow” or “I am working on it now,” and weeks turned into months with nothing actually being done. Despite being reassured that my case was being handled, there was zero movement. Because of this, I nearly ended up in contempt and was lectured by the judge for not filing a motion — the exact thing I hired the firm for and something I had been told over and over was being taken care of. I brought this to the attention of firm leadership and was told they would keep a closer eye on my case. That never happened. More time passed, more billing occurred, and still no progress. I was also billed for things that never happened and accounting never responded when I brought it to their attention.

I eventually hired a different attorney, and meaningful action was taken almost immediately. If you’re okay paying for false promises instead of results, this firm may be fine. Otherwise, I would strongly suggest looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse:
April,

C'mon. You know better than to tell these lies. In fact we both pretty much agreed on all the facts, especially the fact that I was not required to do ANYTHING when the judge went off on both of us. Further, on more than one occassion, I suggested you find a new attorney. I'm fine if you'd like to air out your latest grips after several attorneys before me left your case. I should have left earlier. RED FLAGS. SO SAD.
Chuck

reviews.text:
I hired this law firm to represent me in a custody case against my ex — a situation I’ve unfortunately had to face many times, and every single time he’s been denied. I retained their services and paid around $4,500 upfront, expecting professional guidance and advocacy. Instead, I was told there was nothing they could do for me, that I was essentially screwed, and that I would likely end up paying my ex’s attorney fees in addition to more money owed to them beyond the retainer I had already paid.

I ultimately decided to fire this attorney, represent myself, and I won the case without their help. This experience left me feeling like the firm cared far more about collecting fees than about fighting for their client. I felt unsupported, dismissed, and taken advantage of at a time when I needed real legal representation.

If you’re looking for a lawyer who will truly advocate for you and not just drain your wallet, I strongly recommend looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse: Marena -   I hope you'll have the courage to sign a release allowing us to FULLY and ACCURATELY respond to your misleading review. Even though we worked together for only about 24 hours, we did, and will always advocate for complying with court orders. We're looking forward to you allowing us to elaborate our response.

reviews.text:
I’m leaving this review not as a reflection of your services, but due to the nature of the owner’s responses to client feedback. As someone in business development, I hold communication and professionalism in high regard—especially in difficult situations. I was considering retaining your firm to represent one of my employees, but I was disheartened to see responses that, in my opinion, did not uphold the level of professionalism I would expect from a legal representative.

Even when facing criticism, I believe it's important to respond with composure and respect, rather than engaging at the same tone or level. That kind of maturity builds trust and earns long-term clients.

I sincerely hope to see growth in how communication is handled going forward, and I wish your team the best. Thank you for taking the time to read and consider this feedback.
reviews.ownerResponse: Reviews here are intended to reflect services provided, not your opinion of the responses to client feedback. You seem like an "interesting" person, having reviewed your reviews from Michigan to Yemen to North Carolina. I hope you can find growth and learn to follow rules going forward.

reviews.text:
Would not recommend
Ask Kyle to return  all my reference  letters  by USPS  nothing . I had many issues with Kyle kept changing  what I needed to do
I have multiple sclerosis
Only had a short amount of time to talk with the Judge. Kyle assured  me that we would not have to testify also had a 99percent the court would rule in favor of expungement. Also an issue with mail that was sent to Kyle  not receiving references
reviews.ownerResponse: Mark, as we explain to EVERY person convicted of a crime, asking the court to expunge that conviction is NEVER assured. At no time would we tell you, or anyone with a criminal record, about 99% the court would rule in favor of expungement. That simply isn't true. What is true, is an ongoing list of things unexpectedly arising that require our attorney as well as the assistance of our client. That doesn't mean things "kept changing" it simply means we stay right on top of the case and deal with every detail if it arises. In terms of returning any/all of a file, every client is offered multiple options for receiving the file after a case is done. Mail is one option, but as most people understand, the U S Mail is horrible these days so if something was mailed, maybe you get it and maybe you don't. We have found it much more reliable to email documents to clients since they are quicker and more reliable. At the end of the day, you and we aren't happy with the judge's decision to expunge your record, but we fought this battle as best as we could.

reviews.text: I have had the pleasure of having the Kronzek firm represent me twice. With that said every legal situation is different, lawyers aren’t magicians. I promise you, at least from my 16 months of working with the firm, specifically Kyle Debrucker, that you will get a highly trained lawyer giving you advice that best represents your interests. Sometimes in family law it might be difficult but ask them question and you will get a well thought out answer of why the advised approach is warranted and the dangers or consequences of other approaches. The choice will always be yours they are firm on the belief but work hard to create an approach that meets your objectives. I have never been continuously defended and cared for the way the kronzek firm did. It was a long battle but sticking the line led to a very favorable result for me and the most important person to me my son. Just remember every situation is different and there are processes in all legal situations, and those processes are frustrating but it is the legal system. I promise you that the kronzek firm will work on your behalf better than most (if not the best in the state). Is the kronzek firm cheap? No but when it comes to custody disputes, CPS, divorce and other family matters you want the best and you pay for what you get. That being said they are very transparent with finances and you are billed for service not a flat rate. Any time I need a family lawyer Chuck and Kyle will always get my call. Thanks for the hard work guys!
reviews.ownerResponse: Alec, our team thrives on challenging cases because we understand the importance of family, children and of course, our wonderful clients (like you.) We appreciate you and wish you a healthy, happy, peaceful holiday.

reviews.text: Poor communication, just simply forwards emails from the other party with no context, explanation, or suggestions.  I had to beg for advise on how to proceed with a response. When it’s all done you just handed a stack of paperwork with no organization.
reviews.ownerResponse: Our 23 emails to you confirm lots of communications to you and a review of our invoices to you confirms we absolutely forwarded emails from "the other party" to you to keep you fully informed about your case at all times. Among those 23 emails, we find a great deal of legal advice when appropriate, despite you presenting yourself as a sophisticated business person that understands these things. As is our practice, we give legal advice and we bill for it, when it is warranted. When it is sufficient to simply forward an email without further commentary, we do that without charging our client. Thousands of clients over the decades have thanked us for our approach to careful billing to our clients. Wishing you all the best, Brooks.

reviews.text:
Where to even begin.  I was charged with some serious allegations which if found guilty, the mandatory minimum would have been 25 years.  I started with a court appointed lawyer who I felt wasn’t representing me the best way possible.  So a month before trial, we hired John English of the Kroznek firm.  John was extremely professional and took the case on and quickly caught up to speed, filing more motions and objections in one month than my court appointed had in 6 months.  Most of the objections to the motions that he filed weren’t sustained, or, if we had one to actually go through it was denied at a later date.  But, John English never gave up hope.  He never told me anything he didn’t believe nor did he tell me anything just because he thought I wanted to hear it.  Through a grueling 4 day trial, and jury deliberations for 3-4 hours, the verdict came back.  NOT GUILTY ON ALL CHARGES.  On a previous meeting with John, I had a black and pink striped tie.  He told me that was a cool tie, and I told him if we won it was his.  So after the verdict was in, the tie came off.  I wasn’t even out of the court room yet.  If you’re facing a tough case, do not lose hope.  It’s people like John at the Kronzek Firm that will fight against all odds for your freedom.  When I came home after the verdict my youngest daughter was in tears and said, “Yay! Now I get to have my dad for my birthday!!” Her birthday is next week, and we are forever grateful and thankful to people like John who will bust his tail for his clients.  THANK YOU JOHN FOR ALLOWING ME TO BE THE FATHER MY CHILDREN DESERVE AND A HUSBAND MY WIFE NEEDS.

Jay E
Allegan

reviews.text:
I hired Chuck Kronzek when I had just finished law school, but before I knew what I was doing in a real courtroom. Chuck's cool, relaxed personality is exactly what is needed to ease the tension in a stormy family dispute. I was a nervous wreck, and these days I frequently find myself quoting Chuck's words when I need to calm a client who is anxious about a case. He put me at ease not only with words, but also because he was obviously highly competent.

No matter what fuel the opposing counsel threw on the fire, no matter what cutting words the judge could say, Chuck always had a response that was respectful, composed, and sharply intelligent. If you feel that the opposing party's lawyer or a judge has you under the gun - hire Chuck. If you have a toxic divorce case and need someone to minimize the long-term damage - hire Chuck.

My partner and I eventually got back together and are healthier than ever. Chuck's concentrated efforts to keep things amicable during the divorce played a large part in that door staying open.

I don't know what I would have done without him. Thank you, Chuck.
reviews.ownerResponse: Nothing makes me happier than a happy client, except maybe when a family can be preserved. Wishing you and your family all the best as you move forward with your legal career. (My door is always open to you whenever anything arises.) Thanks for trusting me with your case.

They couldn't win my disability case because an attorney quit, didn't notify any clients, then other attorneys took his cases but did absolutely nothing to prepare the client nor themselves, & lost. Their response, sorry we had a layer quit. They handled an auto accident as well & settled even though I was going into surgery in a couple of weeks & he didn't fight for more because he wanted to just settled the case. Then I was told if I was to sue the individual himself for the accident, they wouldn't represent me. I'd have to find another attorney. They just wanted to settled & move on after collecting their portion. It's a numbers game for them. Take on as many cases as you can, they're bound to win at least a few. So much more. A peer of theirs (different attorney,  different office) made a racist remarks  as we headed into the meeting/settlement/recorded deposition. It was all done on the same day which seemed weird to me because it was all set up to just get a small settlement. I was given no direction & it felt like they were doing me a favor. No respect, was treated as lessor than, & even one of their attorneys, won't name names but I could, confronted me after reading a letter that I sent to his colleague & made a comment about how he disagreed with me, but I stood my ground & told him why I wrote that & that's how I felt & saw the entire situation. The one attorney who lost my disability case was a nice attorney & I believe had no choice but to take my case but it was not a good several years dealing with this law firm. They have relocated since. I just hope that they are better now than they were back then. I wasn't the only person to suffer due to their negligence, many others did as well. I hired abood law firm to help me through my divorce and I couldn't be more disappointed in every level of their service. Lawyers couldn't read and understand paystubs, didn't follow up with items I inquired about, and I have more examples if anyone wants more specifics. I lost my case and ended up paying more in child support than I would have originally.

But the kicker was the billing process. I just got off the phone with Jeff abood and was flabbergasted by what he was saying. In Feb 2023 we came to an agreement on closing out my balance due, they credited me money and I paid the agreed upon final amount due. It's now June 28th, 2023 and I have yet to receive a receipt of the final payment and termination letter. I've been emailing back and forth with Jeff for months trying to get this paperwork and I finally decided to call him. Jeff immediately got defensive and acted like I was crazy for asking for a receipt.  He then tried to claim that my check is my receipt? He then tried to make me feel guilty because they credited my account and how dare I ask for anything else from them. I've honestly never had a conversation quite like the one I had with Jeff. He raised his voice as soon as he could  and clearly doesn't know how to treat customers.

I wouldn't recommend this law firm to anyone. The amount they bill per hour is double what it should be for their level of competency and expertise. Yes they credited my account but that in no way shape or form makes up for everything else I've experienced with them.

If a manager from abood is reading this I'd love to have a conversation.

reviews.text: If I could put 0 stars I would. We were totally scammed out of $2500. We hired Rico Neal to get my husband’s record expunged & he did absolutely nothing. We dealt with them for 2 years. I started calling regularly trying to get some kind of update, with zero response, so I threatened to call the state bar. Then all the sudden the very next day he called my husband & said everything was all set and his record was expunged. Great! Until he applied for a new job & the record was in fact, NOT expunged. We contacted Rico & he tried to act like everything looked fine on his end. Said he’d look into it. Then a few months later, we keep reaching out, now all the sudden he needs records for the application! He NEVER even submitted the application in TWO YEARS. On top of that he blatantly lied to us and said he was all taken care of. Totally insane that anyone would conduct business like this. Do NOT trust this firm with your legal needs.
He’s definitely a beast he got my sentence down from 2 years in prison to a 12 month probation I would highly recommend !!!!! Thank you so much Mr.Neal

His constant honesty with us as clients meant the world—he never sugarcoated anything, and he worked beyond office hours to make sure our needs were met and that everything was handled promptly.

We hired Chris for a very complex CPS/foster care case, and he handled it better than anyone possibly could have. At the end of the day, he proved what needed to be proven and showed his extreme knowledge and skill. He is an absolute master at cross-examination—he has a way of making individuals testifying pause, reflect, and essentially realize, “Wow, I lied and messed up here.” Watching him work was incredible.

The amount we spent on his representation was beyond worth it, and I would make the same decision again without hesitation. Chris is compassionate, dedicated, and extraordinarily skilled. If you need someone who will fight hard for you, support you every step of the way, and bring unmatched expertise to the courtroom, Chris is the one. reviews.text:
Before hiring any attorney, you deserve the truth—not just a star rating.

As of July 2025, Coontz Law shows a 4.7-star average from around 117 Google reviews. (That number doesn’t include any that may have been flagged or removed.) But here’s what the math says that the rating doesn’t:

Of 110 reviewers with public Google profiles, over 40% (about 45 people) had only 1 or 2 reviews total—ever. That’s a huge portion of this firm’s reputation riding on accounts with almost no reviewing history. They may be real clients—but statistically, this isn’t what you’d call a balanced or representative sample.

In my opinion, that alone raises serious questions about how reflective the glowing score really is—especially if you’re considering this firm for a major criminal case.

And if numbers don’t concern you, look at the results: in what appears to be the most high-profile case of Dustyn Coontz’s career, the jury returned a guilty verdict on all charges in less than 40 minutes. The charges carried a potential life sentence. Most juries spend longer picking lunch than they did deciding that outcome.

If you’re facing the most important legal battle of your life, you need more than charisma and curated praise. You need results. Do your homework. Don’t let the star count make the decision for you.

reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Loren, we're so sorry you had this experience. We pride ourselves on being able to get people help quickly. It's fairly common that we hear the exact opposite: that we were the only ones who would jump to action when other firms only want to talk about money. For ethical reasons, we can't talk about the specifics of your inquiry. But what we can say more generally is that sometimes we're focused on getting things right over doing things quickly because we don't want to mislead anyone. When the answers are a little more complex, we do sometimes take more time than needed. That said, we take your feedback seriously and want to improve. If another firm was able to help you more quickly or better, could you let us know who it was so we can pick their brains on their intake process? Or could you let us know how they were able to better serve you? Whatever we can do to avoid making the same mistakes in the future, we want to do it.

Tiffany and her team did an excellent job of handling my case. Very professional and communicative, making the whole process as easy as it could be. Highly recommend.

UPDATE (2/5/26): I hired Ms. DeBruin again for a serious, time-sensitive court matter with high stakes for me and my family. She was direct and honest about risks and outcomes—no false promises—while still being steady and supportive.

She worked hard, communicated consistently, and made sure I was never left in the lurch, including arranging coverage when she was briefly unavailable during a critical period. Ms. DeBruin also valued my input and we worked collaboratively to present the strongest possible case, and she handled communications with the other side effectively.

The result was the best possible outcome I could have hoped for: the matter was dismissed with no penalties. This was a direct result of Ms. DeBruin’s vigorous defense of my rights and her excellent working relationships with court personnel. I highly recommend her for any legal matter.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re thrilled to hear that your experience felt smooth and efficient Sean—that’s always our goal. Navigating the court system can be daunting, so we work hard to ensure our clients feel informed, supported, and confident every step of the way. Your trust meant a great deal, and we’re especially glad our relationships within the court helped move things along more quickly than expected. Wishing you all the best ahead, and thank you again for the strong recommendation!

reviews.text: Was referred by State Bar of Michigan and called for a consultation. Partially spoke with one paralegal and thought it was going well, however the next morning was greeted with a completely different paralegal and came across very crass and cold shoulder. She demanded that I never received the referral from the State Bar and continued to just say that this is not what the office does. I asked politely if they were in Constitutional Law and civil rights. Rather then to receive a polite answer back was given even colder response of this office doesn't have the time of day to even speak to you attitude and call the state bar click....
reviews.ownerResponse:
We sincerely apologize for the experience you had, E Spacelingg. This is not the level of service we strive to provide. Your feedback is important, and we will investigate this matter to ensure improvements are made. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

After further investigation into this matter, we wanted to follow up. We strive to provide excellent customer service. We are deeply troubled by any negative interaction with a person who reaches out to us for legal help. Although we have never worked with a person by the name of E. Spacelingg, we believe we are able to identify him or her based upon a recent interaction. There seems to be some confusion with this reviewer. Contrary to their belief, they were NOT referred to us by the State Bar for the legal matter they described to us. And, after reviewing the information they left with our reception, our staff attorney was able to immediately determine that we are not able to provide legal services for the issue that was described. Proceeding to schedule and conduct a more detailed consultation would have unreasonably delayed the caller in finding appropriate legal help. Our staff politely explained this to them. And we followed up with a written communication explaining all of this in more detail and providing resources to help find an appropriate attorney who can assist. We feel that our response was appropriate and professional. But, again, we truly regret any frustration that this person may have experienced and wish them the best of luck in their future endeavors.

reviews.text:
Possibly a good law office, but our experience was less than par with Midgord. Midgord came unprepared to an emergency exparte hearing, filed allegations and "findings" improperly causing Judge Garcia to be extremely upset, and from the looks of things on the winning plaintiff side, the defense did not even have full proof of evidence and ran off of hearsay.
Did I mention he looks like a frat boy millennial with a man bun? If you just passed the bar, you should have said that upon hiring, sir!!
From presentation, I feel as though a young lawyer not thorough in their findings, cost a defendent a large sum of money, while looking unethical and mediocre to the judge.

Midgord is a joke of a lawyer, with far stretch reaches that any judge could see through. Save your money and hire another lawyer from this law office WITH experience.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your concerns. Firstly, Mr. Midgorden has never represented or counseled you in any capacity. From my understanding, you are the girlfriend of an opposing party in an ongoing custody litigation. We understand completely the hard feelings that come from difficult legal disputes involving a loved one's children. Given the extreme stress that everyone is under in a custody dispute, it is completely understandable, and not at all uncommon, to direct anger towards the attorney representing the side you are against. We remind you, however, that Mr. Midgorden is under the same duty to zealously advocate for his client as the opposing attorney is for his or her client. This can sometimes lead to decisions or actions that may be upsetting or seem unfair from another perspective.
Without discussing the specifics of the case, we believe that the matter you referred to involved a hearing where the Judge simply denied a continuation of an Ex Parte Order. This decision was based on the specific standards for an Emergency Order, which can often differ in interpretation between involved parties.
Your presumption that Mr. Midgorden has 'just passed the bar' is incorrect. He has been licensed to practice law since 2020, the better part of four years. He is dedicated and has handled numerous cases with professionalism and diligence. With all due respect, your personal attacks based upon appearance, and inaccurate assumptions about his experience based upon it, are unreasonable, unfair, and neither reflective of his legal capabilities nor the services provided by our firm.
We respect your perspective and are committed to maintaining the highest standards of professionalism in our practice.

Yes I was deeply disappointed. I called for a very important case of my insurance company cutting my benefits after 18 years. I watched all the videos and I felt that Steven Dramis was definitely the attorney I needed. But the first intake guy told me you didn’t handle cases like mine. I know that was untrue. So I asked to please just get my free consultation and I wanted to speak to Steven. He said he could not do that at the time but offered to call me over the weekend to discuss my case. I found it really uncomfortable and upsetting. Considering I am and still in a terrible situation. So I have sent a dozen emails to Steven. No answer. Not even no I can’t help you nothing. Then I called and it went right to voicemail. I left a voicemail and by that  time I was really upset. I gave all my information. That was almost three weeks ago. I have tried a few more times and nothing. I really felt he was the one to take my case on. But I guess I was wrong because I can’t even get a call back to get a no I can’t help you. I was very disappointed and let down. I honestly don’t think I will ever hear a thing back. So I obviously was wrong. I am now seven months of no benefits with a TBI and my attended care and medical not paid for. Thanks for not even getting the free consultation what is offered on your site. I never knew getting help would be so difficult especially someone in my position. I did end up hearing from Steven Sinus. Who would not help me. I am still in court with an attorney who doesn’t have my best interest at heart. I was forced into a IME and paid for the year they didn’t pay and then they continued to pay for four month and then last month canceled my benefits again based on what their IME doctor was paid over 5000 dollars to say. Now I’m right back at the beginning of hell. If I had a good attorney I would have been protected more. But after numerous times reaching out to Sinas Dramis law for help. I have come to the fact that what say is very untrue.
reviews.ownerResponse: Harmony - This is Stephen Sinas. I am responding to your review directly myself. I believe your comments don’t accurately represent your experience with me. As you know, I have never represented you. Also, during each of your contacts with me and my staff, you were already represented by other attorneys who were actively pursuing a lawsuit on your behalf. That created ethical limitations on how I could work with you. Despite these challenges, I spent a considerable amount of time and effort listening and talking with you about your concerns and trying to help you, as I was able, in the limited capacity. Based on your review, we obviously need to talk again about where things stand and why you are so upset with me. I invite you to call me today at 3 pm so we can discuss everything further. You can call me at 517-394-7500. Can you make that call work today at 3pm? Please let me know.  Thank you.  Sincerely, Stephen Sinas

Thank you so much Kevin for handling my sons case like it was your own, we will forever be grateful!

For three years I had been working to get backpay from SSDI and finally received it. As I had received PIP payments from a No fault motorcycle accident, I had to give all the money received to the insurance Company. Not realizing that they where actually entitled to the total gross amount and not the net that I received. Needless to say,  it was very frustrating. I contacted Ted at the firm and asked if there were any options available to me. Ted advised me that all we could do was appeal to their humanity. He wrote a nice letter to the insurance company with fantastic verbiage and phrasing and lo and behold… they agreed to the amount received! Hallelujah!
Thank you, Thank you, Thank you Ted! This is my second experience with this firm and I couldn’t be more happy with the outcome!

reviews.text:
One of the most important days in the life of our family was the day we hired Tom Sinas of Sinas Dramis Law Firm as our Lawyer.  After the sudden death of my brother David our family was at a loss of what to do. As a suggestion from my Nephew, we interviewed 3 different firms in the Detroit and Grand Rapids area. As we listened, took notes and asked leading questions it was clear who we would follow into battle. Stepping into the board room with this team we felt comfort, strong empathy and consideration as we told the story and listened to a group who had experienced similar scenarios in the past. I was deeply moved as Tom took my sister-in-law aside and had a private conversation with her. Tom’s caring nature would show through as we continued through the 1 ½ year process.
In the beginning of the case, Tom suggested that we create a video to explain the depth of loss we all had through the death of Dave.  The idea felt a little strange at first but we later learned the value of the suggestion. The family met at my mothers house and the videographer  had set us up in the living room. Tom Sinus just had a conversation with each of us and documented the emotional stories from the interview. The videographer  took the raw footage and created a professional document of David's life, family and their loss. When the video was completed it was used as a powerful tool that was sent to the  insurance company. I believe that video helped convince the insurance company of the value of David's life and lead to the compensation asked for without any argument.
As the process is finished and results have been realized, I look back over this time with gratitude. I have never had the cause to include a Law Firm into any of my life experiences. I have to admit I never wanted to have something happen in which I needed one. We could not have imagined that a Lawyer and Law Firm could have been so professional and caring.
Thank you to all of the associates of the firm who helped us through this time of pain and sorrow. As we lay this whole process to rest,  we want to especially thank Tom for his caring and professional attitude toward our case.
Sincerely
Durrell Caister
reviews.ownerResponse: Durrell, thank you so much for kind and thoughtfully written review. It was a pleasure getting to know your family despite the unfortunete circumstance that brought us together. We wish you the best always. - Tom Sinas

reviews.text: If you or a family member is injured in a accident Steve and his team will fight to make sure you receive the benefits you are owed.

reviews.text: Stephen Sinas successfully represented us in a case and we are very happy with his services. He  was compassionate and professional while diligently working on our case to make sure we were taken care properly.

After my three daughters were involved in a head-on collision in November 2019 because of a distracted driver, I contacted Steve Sinas for advice. My oldest daughter, who was 18 at the time, was the driver. She sustained permanent injuries that have altered her life. The driver that hit them was unlicensed (suspended) and uninsured. Our vehicle was a total loss.

I was promptly compensated for the loss of my vehicle, but not for the uninsured motorist benefit. Steve agreed to represent my daughter to get those benefits. After having to file a SOL extension after our insurance company continuously declined to pay out the full benefit, we finally were able to settle for the full benefit amount in November 2023.

Without Steve's help, we never would have gotten the benefits that were rightfully owed to us. Even when the insurance company lowballed their offer, Steve persisted. One deposition from my daughter was all that was needed to resolve this case. Steve went through the deposition process with her to make sure she new what to expect and let her know that he would be with her every step of the way. We're thankful this was resolved without going to trial.

If you need an attorney and are unsure about your situation, reach out to Steve. I'm glad I did.
reviews.ownerResponse: Stephanie, I can't thank you enough for taking the time to leave this thoughtful review. Ensuring my clients receive the benefits they're rightfully owed is our top responsibility. I'm glad I was able to help you resolve this situation, and I wish you and your family the best moving forward. - Stephen Sinas.

Steve and his staff are top-notch. Every call, text, and email was responded to in a timely manner. We never felt our questions were insignificant. Very personable. Real people. Very happy clients here.

reviews.text:
My attorney was Nick, and he would NEVER call you, keep you posted or check on you. Once you signed the retainer, he's hard to find and won't call you back after his receptionist relate your message to him. I called in the mornings and he won't call back til 7:30 pm.. he keep saying "I was in court, I was in court" Yes..@7 P.M. when I here back from him... Workers comp will not talk to you after you hire an attorney, that's why I'm upset... they mailed my info to him that I needed to know about but he didn't tell me, maybe he forgot or He's not taking my case serious! Idk but "THEY" had told me it was sent to him, contact him, and I couldn't get in contact with him to see what was emailed to him, NO RESPONSE! so I was Mad and call back with an attitude today.. Just to tell him about himself, receptionist said he wasn't there, until I got mad then all of a sudden he's about to take my call, he was there all that time y'all!!
.. Reconsider! before signing over your case.. some of them "May NOT!" be bad lawyers but NICK I wouldn't trust...
reviews.ownerResponse:
Eudean,

I contacted you personally to discuss the issues you posted about above. I have reviewed the detailed call log and notes from Nicholas and I see that there has been extensive communication with yourself and Nick and our paralegal Jen. I have counted at least a 10 phone calls with Jen and more than 12 phone calls with Nicholas with detailed notes of the conversations over the last 3 months. Most calls were returned the same day. Claiming that Nicholas never calls you back is just untrue and an unfair depiction of our service.

I explained that Worker's compensation control dates occur in the morning so there are times where or attorneys may spend most of the morning at the Work Comp Bureau and then the rest of the afternoon in appointments. We try to get back to our clients as soon as possible, which is why you likely received calls from Nicholas in the evening. Rather than wait another day, he took his free time to call you back, the same day you called. Calling numerous times in the same day a few hours apart does not typically result in our ability to call you back faster.

As explained on the phone, the last call you made, Nicholas took your call (out of order) immediately after finishing his previous appointment only to be yelled at for being "available" and then hung up on. I hope you can find a different law firm that lives up to your expectations.

As always with workers compensation claims, we do not get paid unless we win your case, and therefore, there is no charge for any of our time. If you have any questions about your case, I would be happy to provide you with a complete copy of your file including the call log and notes or you are welcome to contact me directly.

Sincerely,

Justin Bahrie
Justin@bahrielaw.com

I tried to explain my case several times where he cut me off and made fun of my condition. As he said it wasn't I glad my PTSD just magically went away . Sir joking about someone's medical condition you should know is a big no no. When I stoped him to stand up for my self he tried to cut me off and told me Let me tell you something. No infact sir let me tell you something your not going to talk to me just any way you like . As I didn't make it 5 min talking with this attorney and felt his attitude was ridiculous I would not recommend at all.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Christy,

Sorry to hear about your experience during the intake call. We will try to do better at conveying important information. If you have any follow up questions regarding your disability case, please feel free to reach out to me directly at Justin@bahrielaw.com or call 517-694-1300 and ask for me as I cannot discuss them in a public forum.

When dealing with Social Security, the most relevant medical information is current treatment. If a person has not treated for a particular condition in a significant amount of time, Social Security can pretend that the condition has improved or resolved and is not longer disabling. This is true, even with a properly diagnosed and treated condition in the past. I believe this is what ron was referencing implying the need for current on going treatment with the relevant specialist. I apologize for the disconnect and wish you well.

Sincerely,

Justin Bahrie

reviews.ownerResponse: We appreciate the opportunity to clarify our process. We encourage potential clients to reach out via email or phone to discuss the scope of work and available fee options for their specific needs. If our proposed arrangements don’t align with your expectations, we completely understand your decision to explore other options, as no attorney-client relationship has yet been formed. While we don’t offer free consultations due to our busy schedule, we remain committed to providing quality service. It appears we don’t have a record of you as a client or an individual who has contacted our office, but we’re here to assist if you have any further questions.

reviews.text: If you are in the Lansing area, including Ingham County and the surrounding communities, please consider Pat Gallagher's law firm for your estate planning or probate legal needs. Mr. Gallagher is a trusted colleague of mine whom I've come to know in our capacities as Public Administrators. The Attorney General trusts Pat with administering decedent's estates when there are no other suitable fiduciaries. One of Pat's credentials that stands out is his AV Preeminent rating from Martindale Hubble since 2000. That means he has maintained the highest peer rating for experience, skill and ethical conduct for nearly a quarter Century. Your legal needs will be serviced by a consumate legal professional when you hire his law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words. It is always great to hear this kind of feedback. We are absolutely thrilled to read of your satisfaction with our services.

This started out as a great place and had no complaints. The lawyer I had got a new job and left just before the divorce was finalized. Even though I had an issue with some items in the final judgment, I was told I only had an hour to sign the document. I was told that these can be fixed after the judgement was signed. However, when it came time to change the things, I was told by Carrie Huff that she can no longer help me with the changes. After talking to another lawyer, they wondered why the changes were not made before papers were signed. Now I am spending more money on a different lawyer to get this fixed when it should have been done before everything was over.

Update: It's been 3 months since I've seen my kids. This all could have been prevented when I was at Gallagher Law firm. It's like they don't care about you as a person.

Update: Another day in court. The judge commented that she was displeased with the wording in the final Judgement of divorce. Carrie Huff thought it was in my best interest to sign it without looking out for me. Still paying thousands of dollars to fix her mistake!

reviews.text: They give you a quote for the job. They state it will not be above a certain price unless contested. It was not contested. They charged almost 50 percent more. reviews.ownerResponse: Melody, while we do our best to provide a fee estimate in our initial communications with potential clients, we charge on an hourly basis for most of the work we do as reflected in the written fee agreement signed by the client before we begin any work. Retainer fees primarily serve to guarantee the commitment of the attorney and do not represent the entire cost of a legal process. Sometimes a matter ends up taking less work than estimated and we are able to refund excess retainer fees, and sometimes the entire cost does end up exceeding the upfront fee or retainer. We invoice our clients monthly to keep them informed of the costs incurred. If at any point they are unhappy with their attorneys, clients always have the right to end representation.

reviews.text: Mr. Gallagher responses back to me VERY quickly! Answered all my questions and gave me straight forward answers. He did not just tell me what he thought I wanted to hear. reviews.ownerResponse: Matthew, we understand that it can be frustrating to search for an attorney that is a good fit for your situation. reviews.text:
I contacted this firm, because of google reviews that they were very responsive. That was not my experience at all.

I called on 8/27/19, looking to set up a consultation appointment. Annelise took all of the information from me and wrote it up in an email.  She explained that Mr. Gallagher had been off the previous week and was catching up, but if I had not heard by Thurs to call and check in. I did so with  new additional information. She added that to the original email and explained he had not yet reviewed anything and was still catching up. At that point, I asked if he would just not respond if there was a conflict or if he didn't think he could help. She was very adamant that he would not just leave me hanging. On 9/6/19, I still had not heard anything so I responded to the emails checking in again. As of 9/9/19 I have not received any type of response.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are sorry that you had that experience with your inquiry.
We receive a high volume of calls from individuals seeking representation such as yourself and while we have to put the needs of our current clients first, we generally make a point to respond to each new caller personally as a courtesy, even if it turns out we are unable to assist them.
In your case it is clear there was an oversight which is unacceptable. We have reviewed our recent call logs and made sure all have gotten a response, so you likely have heard back by now. Feel free to reach us by phone or email.
